00030 /*
00031  * faulter.c
00032  *
00033  *      Tries to access an illegal address.
00034  *
00035  * When the system calls assignment is complete, this should run and
00036  * get killed without causing the kernel to panic.
00037  */
00038 
00039 #include <stdio.h>
00040 
00041 #define REALLY_BIG_ADDRESS      0x40000000
00042 
00043 int
00044 main(void) 
00045 {
00046         volatile int i;
00047 
00048         printf("\nEntering the faulter program - I should die immediately\n");
00049         i = *(int *)REALLY_BIG_ADDRESS;    
00050         printf("I didn't get killed!  Program has a bug\n");
00051         return 0;
00052 }
